name: node-base64-js
title: node-base64-js
description:
Base64 encoding/decoding in pure JS
Converts a byte array into base64 string and vice versa.
.
Base64 is binary-to-text encoding schemes that represent binary data
in an ASCII string format by translating it into a radix-64 representation.
(6 bits).
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-base64-js because Base64 encoding/decoding in pure JS.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-base64-js as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-base64-js with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-base64-js --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
